name: Bunch o' Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together 1/2 of the flour with yeast, cardamom, and nutmeg.
2. Heat juice, maple syrup, and butter to body temperature; ensure it is not above 100 degrees Fahrenheit. Add this mixture to the yeast mixture.
3. Stir in orange liqueur.
4. Gradually add the remaining flour until well combined.
5. Turn dough onto a floured work surface and knead for at least 10 minutes.
6. Place dough, oiled side up, in an oiled bowl. Let rise for 1 hour.
7. Punch down dough and let rest for 10 minutes.
8. Remove 1/4 of the dough and set aside.
9. Shape remaining dough into 2-inch balls.
10. Arrange on an oiled cookie sheet in a bunch of grapes shape.
11. Brush each grape ball with egg white and sprinkle with poppy seeds.
12. Use the remaining dough to form grape vine and leaves, attaching them to the top of the grapes.
13. Brush the leaves and stem with egg white.
14. Let the bread rise for an additional 1/2 hour in a warm place.
15. Melt butter and honey together. Slash the grape leaves and brush slashes with the melted mixture.
16. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 20-30 minutes, or until the bread makes a thunking sound when tapped with a knuckle.
